notes: Session-end's user-observable signal: the session row disappears within the expected TTL, and its state immediately before disappearing is ready. Pi supports only the clean-exit teardown variant: headless `pi --print -p <prompt>` runs one turn and then the process exits NATURALLY at turn completion — there is no scripted clean-exit keystroke (the pi interactive driver exposes send/slash/wait_turn/interrupt/sleep only, no exit_clean/sigkill), so variants 2 (SIGKILL idle) and 3 (SIGKILL mid-turn) are out of scope. The daemon observes the session born ready, the working flip on the turn, the working→ready settle when the turn completes (pi's stopReason=stop maps to turn_done), pid_discovered, and finally process_exited AFTER the final ready — with no working/waiting transition between the final ready and the exit, so the state immediately before teardown is ready. KNOWN TIMING RISK: headless pi exits very fast at turn completion, so the post-exit settle the spec asserts may be truncated; if so the recording is committed and the drift flagged, not the spec rebased. teardown anchors to pid_bind (not session_birth) so the matcher picks the post-turn ready arc, not the birth ready.

text: Session appears in ready within 1s of launch
Assistant reply completes → back to ready; state never entered waiting
Headless pi exits at turn completion → process_exited after the final ready; state immediately before disappearing is ready
